title: The Progressives
description: Test your knowledge on the twentieth-century progressive movement!

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Meat scraps were also found being shoveled into receptacles from dirty floors where they were left to lie until again shoveled into barrels or into machines for chopping. These floors, it must be noted, were in most cases damp and soggy, in dark, ill-ventilated rooms, and the employees in utter ignorance of cleanliness or danger to health, expectorated at will upon them. In a word, we saw meat shoveled from filthy wooden floors, piled on tables rarely washed, pushed from room to room in rotten box carts, in all of which processes it was in the way of gathering dirt, splinters, floor filth, and the expectoration of tuberculosis and other diseased workers.”

>-Source: “The Neill-Reynolds Report,” 1906

**Which of the following developments contributed most directly to the conditions described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the lack of federal regulations during the Gilded Age
the opening up of free trade with Canada and Mexico
the draft of factory workers to serve in the Spanish-American War
the passage of the Pure Food and Drug Act

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Meat scraps were also found being shoveled into receptacles from dirty floors where they were left to lie until again shoveled into barrels or into machines for chopping. These floors, it must be noted, were in most cases damp and soggy, in dark, ill-ventilated rooms, and the employees in utter ignorance of cleanliness or danger to health, expectorated at will upon them. In a word, we saw meat shoveled from filthy wooden floors, piled on tables rarely washed, pushed from room to room in rotten box carts, in all of which processes it was in the way of gathering dirt, splinters, floor filth, and the expectoration of tuberculosis and other diseased workers.”

>-Source: “The Neill-Reynolds Report,” 1906

**Which of the following events most directly caused the president to order the investigation reported on in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the publication of *The Jungle*
the passage of the Pure Food and Drugs Act
the creation of the Food and Drug Administration
the passage of the Meat Inspection Act

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Meat scraps were also found being shoveled into receptacles from dirty floors where they were left to lie until again shoveled into barrels or into machines for chopping. These floors, it must be noted, were in most cases damp and soggy, in dark, ill-ventilated rooms, and the employees in utter ignorance of cleanliness or danger to health, expectorated at will upon them. In a word, we saw meat shoveled from filthy wooden floors, piled on tables rarely washed, pushed from room to room in rotten box carts, in all of which processes it was in the way of gathering dirt, splinters, floor filth, and the expectoration of tuberculosis and other diseased workers.”

>-Source: “The Neill-Reynolds Report,” 1906

**The author’s account in the excerpt above most directly encourage which of the following changes in subsequent years?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the expansion of federal power to monitor and regulate the food industry
the rise of a preservation movement to encourage a vegetarian lifestyle
the passage of an amendment giving the president the power to pass regulations on businesses
the increase in federal spending to pay for infrastructure improvements to the meat industry

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Blacksmithing is my trade. And it has always given colour to my view of things. For example, when I was very young, I saw some of the evils in the conditions of life and I wanted to fix them. I couldn’t. There were no tools. We had tools to do almost anything within the shop, beautiful tools, wonderful. And so in other trades, arts and professions; in everything but government. In government, the common trade of all men and the basis of all social life, men worked still with old tools, with old laws, with constitutions and charters which hindered more than they helped. Men suffered from this. There were lawyers enough; many of our ablest men were lawyers. Why didn’t some of them invent legislative implements to help the people govern themselves? Why had we no tool makers for democracy?” 

>-Source: William Simon U’Ren, in [American Magazine, Volume 65](https://play.google.com/store/books/details?id=KblhYqhiWFEC&rdid=book-KblhYqhiWFEC&rdot=1), 1908

**Which of the following historical political parties would be most likely to oppose the views expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Federalists
The Populists
The Transcendentalists
The Jacksonian Democrats

assessment_items.question_content:

I couldn’t. There were no tools. We had tools to do almost anything within the shop, beautiful tools, wonderful. And so in other trades, arts and professions; in everything but government. In government, the common trade of all men and the basis of all social life, men worked still with old tools, with old laws, with constitutions and charters which hindered more than they helped. Men suffered from this. There were lawyers enough; many of our ablest men were lawyers. Why didn’t some of them invent legislative implements to help the people govern themselves? Why had we no tool makers for democracy?” 

>-Source: William Simon U’Ren, in [American Magazine, Volume 65](https://play.google.com/store/books/details?id=KblhYqhiWFEC&rdid=book-KblhYqhiWFEC&rdot=1), 1908

**Which of the following best represents continuity in the years after 1908 with the ideas that the author expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the implementation of a direct election for Senate positions
the ratification of the Eighteenth Amendment
the creation of job training programs to support impoverished workers
the formation of organizations like the NAACP

**Which of the following developments best represents a logical extension of the ideas expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: implementation of the initiative, referendum, and recall in some states
ratification of an amendment to extend suffrage to women and minorities
use of party conventions to elect candidates for the presidential election
institution of literacy tests and poll taxes at polling stations

**Which of the following statements best summarizes the author’s argument in the excerpt above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The current system of government does not allow for broad political participation.
The federal government is too strong and restricts state governments too much.
The state governments are violating civil rights because the Bill of Rights only applies to the federal level.
The current system of government was intervening too much in business practices and negatively affecting the economy.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a86d4274ee1deb7686740759610e400b.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg)

**The image most closely reflects which of the following developments in the political climate in the United States? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased participation of women so that they can advocate for moral reform
increased support for the right to vote to extend to white women
decreased participation of women in the Progressive movement, returning to the “separate spheres” ideology
decreased support for temperance and prohibition as the economy started to suffer

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a86d4274ee1deb7686740759610e400b.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg)

**Which of the following developments is the most direct effect of the situation portrayed in the image? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ratification of the Eighteenth Amendment
extension of suffrage to white women
passage of the Pure Food and Drug Act
formation of the National Woman’s Party

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a86d4274ee1deb7686740759610e400b.jpg)

>-Source: [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Womans-Holy-War.jpg)

**The poster was intended to do which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: promote the Temperance movement
advocate for the passage of the Nineteenth Amendment
convince people to return to Protestantism
persuade women to use birth control

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/659b6f436270a309e99bfc5433cf78c4.jpg)

>-Source: Literary Digest, 1920

**The sentiments expressed in the image helped prompt Congress to take which of the following actions?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ratify the Nineteenth Amendment
pass the Platt Amendment
ratify the Equal Rights Amendment
pass the Fifteenth Amendment

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/659b6f436270a309e99bfc5433cf78c4.jpg)

>-Source: Literary Digest, 1920

**Which of the following best describes the message of the political cartoon?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Amendments are often difficult to ratify.
Support for women’s suffrage is waning.
Amendments do not cause any real change.
Support for banning voting restrictions is increasing.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fa7b017d816dfcbcabd455af91cd08f.png)

>-Source: Udo J. Keppler, “Who’ll stand by him?” [Library of Congress](http://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2011647504/), 1909

**Which of the following groups would have been most likely to support the sentiment of the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Conservationists
Suffragettes
Monopolists
Populists

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fa7b017d816dfcbcabd455af91cd08f.png)

>-Source: Udo J. Keppler, “Who’ll stand by him?” [Library of Congress](http://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2011647504/), 1909

**The image was created most directly in response to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rapid pace and wasteful practices of the lumber industry
a series of fires that were not addressed because there were no fire departments
a scandal where the government secretly sold Teapot Dome Petroleum Reserves
the release of the popular investigative novel, *The Jungle*

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7fa7b017d816dfcbcabd455af91cd08f.png)

>-Source: Udo J. Keppler, “Who’ll stand by him?” [Library of Congress](http://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2011647504/), 1909

**The ideas expressed through the image reveal that in 1909, which of the following was most true of the United States?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of a conservation movement that shifted the priorities of the federal government
the rise in investigative journalism shined a light on corruption in the federal government
the lack of centralization of federal resources meant that local governments had to handle national issues
the lack of laws to protect the environment have led to a malnourished and unhealthy American population
